fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Connecticut Pharmacist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a pharmacist? Here's whether your license transfers to Connecticut - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: pharmacist license reciprocity connecticut, how to transfer pharmacist license to connecticut, pharmacist compact connecticut, connecticut pharmacist endorsement application

board.name: Connecticut Commission of Pharmacy
board.mailing_address: Connecticut Department of Public Health, Practitioner Licensing and Investigations Section, 410 Capitol Ave., MS #12PL, P.O. Box 340308, Hartford, CT 06134

license_types.title: RPh, PharmD
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a pharmacist in Connecticut.

steps.title: Create an Account in eLicensing
steps.description: Register and create an account on the Connecticut eLicensing System to begin your application process.

steps.title: Start a New Pharmacist Application
steps.description: Log into your eLicensing account, navigate to 'New Application', and select the appropriate application type, such as 'Pharmacist - Endorsement' or 'Pharmacist (New)'.

steps.title: Complete Application and Pay Fee
steps.description: Fill out the online application form completely and submit the non-refundable $200 application fee through the Connecticut eLicensing System.

steps.title: Submit Required Documentation
steps.description: Arrange for NABP to transfer your NAPLEX and MPJE scores to the Connecticut DPH. Additionally, submit verifications of licensure from all other states where you hold or have held a pharmacist license, and any required internship hour documentation.

steps.title: Await Application Review
steps.description: The Department of Public Health will review your complete application and all supporting documents. You can monitor the status of your application via your eLicensing account.

steps.title: License Issuance
steps.description: Upon approval, your Connecticut Pharmacist license will be issued. You will typically receive notification and can view or print your license directly from the eLicensing system.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NAPLEX and MPJE.

provisional_text: Some states offer an intern or provisional license for graduates actively accumulating required hours or waiting to take the NAPLEX.
reciprocity_text: Pharmacists licensed in another state can often apply via license transfer (reciprocity) through the NABP, though most states still require passing their specific MPJE.

faqs.question: How much does a Connecticut pharmacist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies by state. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is the MPJE required in all states?
faqs.answer: Almost all states require passing their state-specific Multistate Pharmacy Jurisprudence Examination (MPJE) to verify knowledge of local pharmacy laws.

faqs.question: Does Connecticut have reciprocity for pharmacists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacist practice in Connecticut. To work in Connecticut, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacist license to Connecticut?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Connecticut while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Pharmacists licensed in another state can often apply via license transfer (reciprocity) through the NABP, though most states still require passing their specific MPJE.

hero.title: Become a Pharmacist in Connecticut
hero.dek: Pharmacist applicants for licensure in Connecticut must apply online through the Connecticut eLicensing System, which is managed by the Department of Public Health.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps: To create an account, visit the Connecticut eLicensing System and click 'Register' at the top right of the page. Follow the prompts to enter your personal information, create a username and password, and verify your email. Once registered, log in and select 'New Application' to begin your pharmacist application.
